title: bilabrella involuta
intro: Bilabrella involuta, also known as the Bilabrella plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is native to Western Australia, where it grows in sandy soils and has been used by the local Indigenous people for its medicinal and culinary properties. Bilabrella involuta has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots can be used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which have been reported to provide relief from pain, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
